title: oxalis reclinata var. micromera
intro: Oxalis reclinata var. micromera, also known as the creeping wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Oxalidaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oxalis reclinata var. micromera include its potential to treat conditions such as urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural diuretic to aid in fluid retention. Herbal preparations of Oxalis reclinata var. micromera include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
